class Database:
    """Thread-safe SQLite database with transaction support and backup/recovery."""

    def __init__(self, db_path: str = ":memory:"):
        self.db_path = db_path
        self._local = threading.local()
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._initialize_schema()

    def _get_connection(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
        if not hasattr(self._local, "connection") or self._local.connection is None:
            self._local.connection = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path, check_same_thread=False)
            self._local.connection.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
            self._local.connection.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
            self._local.connection.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON")
        return self._local.connection

    @contextmanager
    def transaction(self):
        conn = self._get_connection()
        with self._lock:
            try:
                yield conn
                conn.commit()
            except Exception:
                conn.rollback()
                raise

    def execute(self, query: str, params: tuple = ()) -> list:
        with self.transaction() as conn:
            cursor = conn.execute(query, params)
            return [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]

    def execute_write(self, query: str, params: tuple = ()) -> int:
        with self.transaction() as conn:
            cursor = conn.execute(query, params)
            return cursor.lastrowid

    def _initialize_schema(self):
        schema = """
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
            id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
            username TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
            email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
            password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
            full_name TEXT NOT NULL,
            role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member',
            department TEXT,
            is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
            created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
            updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
        );

        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS projects (
            id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
            name TEXT NOT NULL,
            description TEXT,
            status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'planning',
            priority TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium',
            owner_id TEXT NOT NULL,
            department TEXT,
            start_date TEXT,
            end_date TEXT,
            budget REAL DEFAULT 0.0,
            created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
            updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (owner_id) REFERENCES users(id)
        );

        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (
            id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
            project_id TEXT NOT NULL,
            title TEXT NOT NULL,
            description TEXT,
            status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'todo',
            priority TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium',
            assigned_to TEXT,
            created_by TEXT NOT NULL,
            estimated_hours REAL DEFAULT 0.0,
            actual_hours REAL DEFAULT 0.0,
            due_date TEXT,
            completed_at TEXT,
            created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
            updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES projects(id),
            FOREIGN KEY (assigned_to) REFERENCES users(id),
            FOREIGN KEY (created_by) REFERENCES users(id)
        );

        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_dependencies (
            task_id TEXT NOT NULL,
            depends_on TEXT NOT NULL,
            PRIMARY KEY (task_id, depends_on),
            FOREIGN KEY (task_id) REFERENCES tasks(id),
            FOREIGN KEY (depends_on) REFERENCES tasks(id)
        );

        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS resources (
            id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
            user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
            project_id TEXT NOT NULL,
            allocation_percent REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 100.0,
            role_in_project TEXT,
            start_date TEXT,
            end_date TEXT,
            created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),
            FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES projects(id)
        );

        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS messages (
            id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
            project_id TEXT,
            sender_id TEXT NOT NULL,
            recipient_id TEXT,
            channel TEXT DEFAULT 'general',
            content TEXT NOT NULL,
            message_type TEXT DEFAULT 'text',
            is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
            created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES projects(id),
            FOREIGN KEY (sender_id) REFERENCES users(id)
        );

        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS activity_log (
            id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
            user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
            action TEXT NOT NULL,
            entity_type TEXT NOT NULL,
            entity_id TEXT,
            details TEXT,
            created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
        );

        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS integrations (
            id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
            user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
            service_type TEXT NOT NULL,
            config TEXT NOT NULL,
            is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
            created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
        );

        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (
            id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
            user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
            title TEXT NOT NULL,
            message TEXT NOT NULL,
            notification_type TEXT DEFAULT 'info',
            is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
            created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
        );

        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_project ON tasks(project_id);
        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_assigned ON tasks(assigned_to);
        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_status ON tasks(status);
        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_project ON messages(project_id);
        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_sender ON messages(sender_id);
        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_resources_user ON resources(user_id);
        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_resources_project ON resources(project_id);
        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_activity_user ON activity_log(user_id);
        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user ON notifications(user_id);
        """
        conn = self._get_connection()
        conn.executescript(schema)
        conn.commit()

    def backup(self, backup_path: str) -> bool:
        try:
            source = self._get_connection()
            dest = sqlite3.connect(backup_path)
            source.backup(dest)
            dest.close()
            logger.info(f"Database backed up to {backup_path}")
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Backup failed: {e}")
            return False

    def restore(self, backup_path: str) -> bool:
        try:
            backup_conn = sqlite3.connect(backup_path)
            conn = self._get_connection()
            backup_conn.backup(conn)
            backup_conn.close()
            logger.info(f"Database restored from {backup_path}")
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Restore failed: {e}")
            return False

class AuthService:
    """Handles authentication, session management, and role-based access control."""

    ROLE_PERMISSIONS = {
        UserRole.ADMIN.value: {
            "users": ["create", "read", "update", "delete"],
            "projects": ["create", "read", "update", "delete"],
            "tasks": ["create", "read", "update", "delete"],
            "reports": ["create", "read"],
            "integrations": ["create", "read", "update", "delete"],
            "messages": ["create", "read", "delete"],
        },
        UserRole.MANAGER.value: {
            "users": ["read"],
            "projects": ["create", "read", "update"],
            "tasks": ["create", "read", "update", "delete"],
            "reports": ["create", "read"],
            "integrations": ["create", "read", "update"],
            "messages": ["create", "read"],
        },
        UserRole.MEMBER.value: {
            "users": ["read"],
            "projects": ["read"],
            "tasks": ["create", "read", "update"],
            "reports": ["read"],
            "integrations": ["read"],
            "messages": ["create", "read"],
        },
        UserRole.VIEWER.value: {
            "users": ["read"],
            "projects": ["read"],
            "tasks": ["read"],
            "reports": ["read"],
            "integrations": [],
            "messages": ["read"],
        },
    }

    def __init__(self, db: Database):
        self.db = db
        self._sessions: dict[str, dict] = {}

    @staticmethod
    def hash_password(password: str) -> str:
        salt = secrets.token_hex(16)
        hashed = hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac("sha256", password.encode(), salt.encode(), 100000)
        return f"{salt}:{hashed.hex()}"

    @staticmethod
    def verify_password(password: str, password_hash: str) -> bool:
        try:
            salt, hashed = password_hash.split(":")
            expected = hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac("sha256", password.encode(), salt.encode(), 100000)
            return expected.hex() == hashed
        except (ValueError, AttributeError):
            return False

    def register_user(self, username: str, email: str, password: str,
                      full_name: str, role: str = "member", department: str = "") -> dict:
        existing = self.db.execute(
            "SELECT id FROM users WHERE username = ? OR email = ?", (username, email)
        )
        if existing:
            return {"success": False, "error": "Username or email already exists"}

        user = User(
            username=username,
            email=email,
            password_hash=self.hash_password(password),
            full_name=full_name,
            role=role,
            department=department,
        )
        self.db.execute_write(
            """INSERT INTO users (id, username, email, password_hash, full_name,
               role, department, is_active, created_at, updated_at)
               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
            (user.id, user.username, user.email, user.password_hash,
             user.full_name, user.role, user.department, 1,
             user.created_at, user.updated_at),
        )
        logger.info(f"User registered: {username}")
        return {"success": True, "user_id": user.id}

    def login(self, username: str, password: str) -> dict:
        rows = self.db.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ? AND is_active = 1", (username,)
        )
        if not rows:
            return {"success": False, "error": "Invalid credentials"}

        user = rows[0]
        if not self.verify_password(password, user["password_hash"]):
            return {"success": False, "error": "Invalid credentials"}

        token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
        self._sessions[token] = {
            "user_id": user["id"],
            "username": user["username"],
            "role": user["role"],
            "department": user["department"],
            "logged_in_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        }
        return {"success": True, "token": token, "user_id": user["id"], "role": user["role"]}

    def logout(self, token: str) -> bool:
        return self._sessions.pop(token, None) is not None

    def get_session(self, token: str) -> Optional[dict]:
        return self._sessions.get(token)

    def check_permission(self, token: str, resource: str, action: str) -> bool:
        session = self.get_session(token)
        if not session:
            return False
        role = session["role"]
        perms = self.ROLE_PERMISSIONS.get(role, {})
        return action in perms.get(resource, [])

class TaskManager:
    """Handles task CRUD, assignment, status tracking, and dependency management."""

    def __init__(self, db: Database):
        self.db = db

    def create_task(self, project_id: str, title: str, created_by: str,
                    description: str = "", priority: str = "medium",
                    assigned_to: str = "", estimated_hours: float = 0.0,
                    due_date: str = "") -> dict:
        task = Task(
            project_id=project_id,
            title=title,
            description=description,
            priority=priority,
            assigned_to=assigned_to,
            created_by=created_by,
            estimated_hours=estimated_hours,
            due_date=due_date,
        )
        self.db.execute_write(
            """INSERT INTO tasks (id, project_id, title, description, status, priority,
               assigned_to, created_by, estimated_hours, actual_hours, due_date,
               completed_at, created_at, updated_at)
               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
            (task.id, task.project_id, task.title, task.description, task.status,
             task.priority, task.assigned_to, task.created_by, task.estimated_hours,
             task.actual_hours, task.due_date, task.completed_at,
             task.created_at, task.updated_at),
        )
        self._log_activity(created_by, "create_task", "task", task.id, f"Created task: {title}")
        return {"success": True, "task_id": task.id}

    def update_task(self, task_id: str, user_id: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
        allowed = {"title", "description", "status", "priority", "assigned_to",
                   "estimated_hours", "actual_hours", "due_date"}
        updates = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k in allowed and v is not None}
        if not updates:
            return {"success": False, "error": "No valid fields to update"}

        if "status" in updates and updates["status"] == TaskStatus.DONE.value:
            updates["completed_at"] = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()

        updates["updated_at"] = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
        set_clause = ", ".join(f"{k} = ?" for k in updates)
        values = list(updates.values()) + [task_id]

        self.db.execute_write(f"UPDATE tasks SET {set_clause} WHERE id = ?", tuple(values))
        self._log_activity(user_id, "update_task", "task", task_id, json.dumps(updates))
        return {"success": True, "task_id": task_id}

    def get_task(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:
        rows = self.db.execute("SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE id = ?", (task_id,))
        return rows[0] if rows else None

    def get_tasks_by_project(self, project_id: str, status: str = None) -> list:
        if status:
            return self.db.execute(
                "SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE project_id = ? AND status = ? ORDER BY priority, due_date",
                (project_id, status),
            )
        return self.db.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE project_id = ? ORDER BY priority, due_date",
            (project_id,),
        )

    def get_tasks_by_user(self, user_id: str, status: str = None) -> list:
        if status:
            return self.db.execute(
                "SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE assigned_to = ? AND status = ? ORDER BY priority, due_date",
                (user_id, status),
            )
        return self.db.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE assigned_to = ? ORDER BY priority, due_date",
            (user_id,),
        )

    def add_dependency(self, task_id: str, depends_on: str) -> dict:
        if self._would_create_cycle(task_id, depends_on):
            return {"success": False, "error": "Dependency would create a cycle"}
        self.db.execute_write(
            "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO task_dependencies (task_id, depends_on) VALUES (?, ?)",
            (task_id, depends_on),
        )
        return {"success": True}

    def get_dependencies(self, task_id: str) -> list:
        return self.db.execute(
            """SELECT t.* FROM tasks t
               JOIN task_dependencies td ON t.id = td.depends_on
               WHERE td.task_id = ?""",
            (task_id,),
        )

    def delete_task(self, task_id: str, user_id: str) -> dict:
        self.db.execute_write("DELETE FROM task_dependencies WHERE task_id = ? OR depends_on = ?",
                             (task_id, task_id))
        self.db.execute_write("DELETE FROM tasks WHERE id = ?", (task_id,))
        self._log_activity(user_id, "delete_task", "task", task_id, "Task deleted")
        return {"success": True}

    def _would_create_cycle(self, task_id: str, depends_on: str) -> bool:
        visited = set()
        queue = [depends_on]
        while queue:
            current = queue.pop(0)
            if current == task_id:
                return True
            if current in visited:
                continue
            visited.add(current)
            deps = self.db.execute(
                "SELECT depends_on FROM task_dependencies WHERE task_id = ?", (current,)
            )
            queue.extend(d["depends_on"] for d in deps)
        return False

    def _log_activity(self, user_id: str, action: str, entity_type: str,
                      entity_id: str, details: str):
        self.db.execute_write(
            """INSERT INTO activity_log (id, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, details, created_at)
               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
            (str(uuid.uuid4()), user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id,
             details, datetime.utcnow().isoformat()),
        )

class ProjectManager:
    """Handles project lifecycle, status tracking, and team coordination."""

    def __init__(self, db: Database):
        self.db = db

    def create_project(self, name: str, owner_id: str, description: str = "",
                       priority: str = "medium", department: str = "",
                       start_date: str = "", end_date: str = "",
                       budget: float = 0.0) -> dict:
        project = Project(
            name=name,
            description=description,
            priority=priority,
            owner_id=owner_id,
            department=department,
            start_date=start_date,
            end_date=end_date,
            budget=budget,
        )
        self.db.execute_write(
            """INSERT INTO projects (id, name, description, status, priority, owner_id,
               department, start_date, end_date, budget, created_at, updated_at)
               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
            (project.id, project.name, project.description, project.status,
             project.priority, project.owner_id, project.department,
             project.start_date, project.end_date, project.budget,
             project.created_at, project.updated_at),
        )
        return {"success": True, "project_id": project.id}

    def update_project(self, project_id: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
        allowed = {"name", "description", "status", "priority", "department",
                   "start_date", "end_date", "budget"}
        updates = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k in allowed and v is not None}
        if not updates:
            return {"success": False, "error": "No valid fields to update"}

        updates["updated_at"] = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
        set_clause = ", ".join(f"{k} = ?" for k in updates)
        values = list(updates.values()) + [project_id]
        self.db.execute_write(f"UPDATE projects SET {set_clause} WHERE id = ?", tuple(values))
        return {"success": True, "project_id": project_id}

    def get_project(self, project_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:
        rows = self.db.execute("SELECT * FROM projects WHERE id = ?", (project_id,))
        return rows[0] if rows else None

    def list_projects(self, department: str = None, status: str = None) -> list:
        query = "SELECT * FROM projects WHERE 1=1"
        params = []
        if department:
            query += " AND department = ?"
            params.append(department)
        if status:
            query += " AND status = ?"
            params.append(status)
        query += " ORDER BY priority, created_at DESC"
        return self.db.execute(query, tuple(params))

    def delete_project(self, project_id: str) -> dict:
        self.db.execute_write("DELETE FROM tasks WHERE project_id = ?", (project_id,))
        self.db.execute_write("DELETE FROM resources WHERE project_id = ?", (project_id,))
        self.db.execute_write("DELETE FROM messages WHERE project_id = ?", (project_id,))
        self.db.execute_write("DELETE FROM projects WHERE id = ?", (project_id,))
        return {"success": True}

    def get_project_summary(self, project_id: str) -> dict:
        project = self.get_project(project_id)
        if not project:
            return {"error": "Project not found"}

        tasks = self.db.execute("SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count FROM tasks WHERE project_id = ? GROUP BY status", (project_id,))
        resources = self.db.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM resources WHERE project_id = ?", (project_id,))
        task_counts = {row["status"]: row["count"] for row in tasks}
        total_tasks = sum(task_counts.values())
        done_tasks = task_counts.get("done", 0)

        return {
            "project": project,
            "task_counts": task_counts,
            "total_tasks": total_tasks,
            "completion_percent": round((done_tasks / total_tasks * 100) if total_tasks > 0 else 0, 1),
            "team_size": resources[0]["count"] if resources else 0,
        }

class ResourceManager:
    """Manages resource allocation, availability tracking, and workload balancing."""

    def __init__(self, db: Database):
        self.db = db

    def allocate_resource(self, user_id: str, project_id: str,
                          allocation_percent: float = 100.0,
                          role_in_project: str = "", start_date: str = "",
                          end_date: str = "") -> dict:
        current = self._get_total_allocation(user_id)
        if current + allocation_percent > 100.0:
            return {
                "success": False,
                "error": f"Over-allocation: user already at {current}%",
                "available": 100.0 - current,
            }

        resource_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.db.execute_write(
            """INSERT INTO resources (id, user_id, project_id, allocation_percent,
               role_in_project, start_date, end_date, created_at)
               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
            (resource_id, user_id, project_id, allocation_percent,
             role_in_project, start_date, end_date, datetime.utcnow().isoformat()),
        )
        return {"success": True, "resource_id": resource_id}

    def deallocate_resource(self, resource_id: str) -> dict:
        self.db.execute_write("DELETE FROM resources WHERE id = ?", (resource_id,))
        return {"success": True}

    def update_allocation(self, resource_id: str, allocation_percent: float) -> dict:
        rows = self.db.execute("SELECT * FROM resources WHERE id = ?", (resource_id,))
        if not rows:
            return {"success": False, "error": "Resource allocation not found"}

        resource = rows[0]
        current_total = self._get_total_allocation(resource["user_id"])
        available = 100.0 - current_total + resource["allocation_percent"]

        if allocation_percent > available:
            return {"success": False, "error": f"Cannot allocate {allocation_percent}%, only {available}% available"}

        self.db.execute_write(
            "UPDATE resources SET allocation_percent = ? WHERE id = ?",
            (allocation_percent, resource_id),
        )
        return {"success": True}

    def get_user_allocations(self, user_id: str) -> list:
        return self.db.execute(
            """SELECT r.*, p.name as project_name FROM resources r
               JOIN projects p ON r.project_id = p.id
               WHERE r.user_id = ?""",
            (user_id,),
        )

    def get_project_team(self, project_id: str) -> list:
        return self.db.execute(
            """SELECT r.*, u.full_name, u.username, u.department FROM resources r
               JOIN users u ON r.user_id = u.id
               WHERE r.project_id = ?""",
            (project_id,),
        )

    def get_availability(self, user_id: str) -> dict:
        total = self._get_total_allocation(user_id)
        allocations = self.get_user_allocations(user_id)
        return {
            "user_id": user_id,
            "total_allocated": total,
            "available": 100.0 - total,
            "allocations": allocations,
        }

    def get_workload_report(self) -> list:
        users = self.db.execute("SELECT id, full_name, department FROM users WHERE is_active = 1")
        report = []
        for user in users:
            alloc = self._get_total_allocation(user["id"])
            task_count = self.db.execute(
                "SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM tasks WHERE assigned_to = ? AND status != 'done'",
                (user["id"],),
            )
            report.append({
                "user_id": user["id"],
                "full_name": user["full_name"],
                "department": user["department"],
                "allocation_percent": alloc,
                "active_tasks": task_count[0]["count"] if task_count else 0,
            })
        return sorted(report, key=lambda x: x["allocation_percent"], reverse=True)

    def _get_total_allocation(self, user_id: str) -> float:
        rows = self.db.execute(
            "SELECT COALESCE(SUM(allocation_percent), 0) as total FROM resources WHERE user_id = ?",
            (user_id,),
        )
        return rows[0]["total"] if rows else 0.0

class CommunicationService:
    """Handles messaging, notifications, and real-time communication channels."""

    def __init__(self, db: Database):
        self.db = db
        self._subscribers: dict[str, list] = defaultdict(list)

    def send_message(self, sender_id: str, content: str, project_id: str = "",
                     recipient_id: str = "", channel: str = "general",
                     message_type: str = "text") -> dict:
        msg = Message(
            project_id=project_id,
            sender_id=sender_id,
            recipient_id=recipient_id,
            channel=channel,
            content=content,
            message_type=message_type,
        )
        self.db.execute_write(
            """INSERT INTO messages (id, project_id, sender_id, recipient_id,
               channel, content, message_type, is_read, created_at)
               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
            (msg.id, msg.project_id, msg.sender_id, msg.recipient_id,
             msg.channel, msg.content, msg.message_type, 0, msg.created_at),
        )
        self._notify_subscribers(channel, msg)

        if recipient_id:
            self._create_notification(
                recipient_id, "New Message",
                f"You have a new message from {sender_id}", "message",
            )
        return {"success": True, "message_id": msg.id}

    def get_messages(self, project_id: str = "", channel: str = "general",
                     limit: int = 50, offset: int = 0) -> list:
        if project_id:
            return self.db.execute(
                """SELECT m.*, u.full_name as sender_name FROM messages m
                   JOIN users u ON m.sender_id = u.id
                   WHERE m.project_id = ? AND m.channel = ?
                   ORDER BY m.created_at DESC LIMIT ? OFFSET ?""",
                (project_id, channel, limit, offset),
            )
        return self.db.execute(
            """SELECT m.*, u.full_name as sender_name FROM messages m
               JOIN users u ON m.sender_id = u.id
               WHERE m.channel = ?
               ORDER BY m.created_at DESC LIMIT ? OFFSET ?""",
            (channel, limit, offset),
        )

    def get_direct_messages(self, user_id: str, other_user_id: str, limit: int = 50) -> list:
        return self.db.execute(
            """SELECT m.*, u.full_name as sender_name FROM messages m
               JOIN users u ON m.sender_id = u.id
               WHERE (m.sender_id = ? AND m.recipient_id = ?)
                  OR (m.sender_id = ? AND m.recipient_id = ?)
               ORDER BY m.created_at DESC LIMIT ?""",
            (user_id, other_user_id, other_user_id, user_id, limit),
        )

    def mark_as_read(self, message_id: str) -> dict:
        self.db.execute_write("UPDATE messages SET is_read = 1 WHERE id = ?", (message_id,))
        return {"success": True}

    def get_unread_count(self, user_id: str) -> int:
        rows = self.db.execute(
            "SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM messages WHERE recipient_id = ? AND is_read = 0",
            (user_id,),
        )
        return rows[0]["count"] if rows else 0

    def subscribe(self, channel: str, callback):
        self._subscribers[channel].append(callback)

    def _notify_subscribers(self, channel: str, message: Message):
        for callback in self._subscribers.get(channel, []):
            try:
                callback(message)
            except Exception as e:
                logger.error(f"Subscriber notification failed: {e}")

    def get_notifications(self, user_id: str, unread_only: bool = False) -> list:
        query = "SELECT * FROM notifications WHERE user_id = ?"
        params = [user_id]
        if unread_only:
            query += " AND is_read = 0"
        query += " ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 50"
        return self.db.execute(query, tuple(params))

    def mark_notification_read(self, notification_id: str) -> dict:
        self.db.execute_write("UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE id = ?", (notification_id,))
        return {"success": True}

    def _create_notification(self, user_id: str, title: str, message: str,
                             notification_type: str = "info"):
        self.db.execute_write(
            """INSERT INTO notifications (id, user_id, title, message, notification_type, is_read, created_at)
               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 0, ?)""",
            (str(uuid.uuid4()), user_id, title, message, notification_type,
             datetime.utcnow().isoformat()),
        )

class IntegrationService:
    """Manages integrations with calendar, email, CRM, and other third-party tools."""

    SUPPORTED_SERVICES = {
        "calendar": {"name": "Calendar", "fields": ["api_key", "calendar_id", "provider"]},
        "email": {"name": "Email Service", "fields": ["smtp_host", "smtp_port", "username", "password"]},
        "crm": {"name": "CRM System", "fields": ["api_key", "base_url", "org_id"]},
        "slack": {"name": "Slack", "fields": ["webhook_url", "channel", "bot_token"]},
        "jira": {"name": "Jira", "fields": ["api_key", "base_url", "project_key"]},
    }

    def __init__(self, db: Database):
        self.db = db

    def add_integration(self, user_id: str, service_type: str, config: dict) -> dict:
        if service_type not in self.SUPPORTED_SERVICES:
            return {"success": False, "error": f"Unsupported service: {service_type}"}

        integration_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.db.execute_write(
            """INSERT INTO integrations (id, user_id, service_type, config, is_active, created_at)
               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 1, ?)""",
            (integration_id, user_id, service_type, json.dumps(config),
             datetime.utcnow().isoformat()),
        )
        return {"success": True, "integration_id": integration_id}

    def remove_integration(self, integration_id: str) -> dict:
        self.db.execute_write("DELETE FROM integrations WHERE id = ?", (integration_id,))
        return {"success": True}

    def get_integrations(self, user_id: str) -> list:
        rows = self.db.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM integrations WHERE user_id = ? AND is_active = 1", (user_id,)
        )
        for row in rows:
            row["config"] = json.loads(row["config"])
        return rows

    def sync_calendar_event(self, user_id: str, event_data: dict) -> dict:
        integrations = self.db.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM integrations WHERE user_id = ? AND service_type = 'calendar' AND is_active = 1",
            (user_id,),
        )
        if not integrations:
            return {"success": False, "error": "No calendar integration configured"}

        logger.info(f"Calendar sync for user {user_id}: {event_data.get('title', 'Untitled')}")
        return {"success": True, "synced": True, "event": event_data}

    def send_email_notification(self, user_id: str, to: str, subject: str, body: str) -> dict:
        integrations = self.db.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM integrations WHERE user_id = ? AND service_type = 'email' AND is_active = 1",
            (user_id,),
        )
        if not integrations:
            return {"success": False, "error": "No email integration configured"}

        logger.info(f"Email sent to {to}: {subject}")
        return {"success": True, "sent": True, "to": to, "subject": subject}

    def sync_crm_contact(self, user_id: str, contact_data: dict) -> dict:
        integrations = self.db.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM integrations WHERE user_id = ? AND service_type = 'crm' AND is_active = 1",
            (user_id,),
        )
        if not integrations:
            return {"success": False, "error": "No CRM integration configured"}

        logger.info(f"CRM sync for user {user_id}: {contact_data.get('name', 'Unknown')}")
        return {"success": True, "synced": True, "contact": contact_data}

    def get_supported_services(self) -> dict:
        return self.SUPPORTED_SERVICES

class ReportingService:
    """Generates project progress, resource utilization, and performance reports."""

    def __init__(self, db: Database):
        self.db = db

    def project_progress_report(self, project_id: str) -> dict:
        project = self.db.execute("SELECT * FROM projects WHERE id = ?", (project_id,))
        if not project:
            return {"error": "Project not found"}
        project = project[0]

        tasks = self.db.execute("SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE project_id = ?", (project_id,))
        total = len(tasks)
        by_status = defaultdict(int)
        total_estimated = 0.0
        total_actual = 0.0
        overdue = 0

        now = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
        for t in tasks:
            by_status[t["status"]] += 1
            total_estimated += t["estimated_hours"] or 0
            total_actual += t["actual_hours"] or 0
            if t["due_date"] and t["due_date"] < now and t["status"] != "done":
                overdue += 1

        done = by_status.get("done", 0)
        completion = round((done / total * 100) if total > 0 else 0, 1)
        efficiency = round((total_estimated / total_actual * 100) if total_actual > 0 else 0, 1)

        return {
            "report_type": "project_progress",
            "generated_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            "project": {"id": project["id"], "name": project["name"], "status": project["status"]},
            "metrics": {
                "total_tasks": total,
                "tasks_by_status": dict(by_status),
                "completion_percent": completion,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "total_estimated_hours": total_estimated,
                "total_actual_hours": total_actual,
                "efficiency_percent": efficiency,
            },
            "timeline": {
                "start_date": project["start_date"],
                "end_date": project["end_date"],
                "budget": project["budget"],
            },
        }

    def resource_utilization_report(self, department: str = None) -> dict:
        query = """
            SELECT u.id, u.full_name, u.department,
                   COALESCE(SUM(r.allocation_percent), 0) as total_allocation
            FROM users u
            LEFT JOIN resources r ON u.id = r.user_id
            WHERE u.is_active = 1
        """
        params = []
        if department:
            query += " AND u.department = ?"
            params.append(department)
        query += " GROUP BY u.id, u.full_name, u.department"

        users = self.db.execute(query, tuple(params))
        total_capacity = len(users) * 100.0
        total_used = sum(u["total_allocation"] for u in users)
        underutilized = [u for u in users if u["total_allocation"] < 50]
        overutilized = [u for u in users if u["total_allocation"] > 100]

        return {
            "report_type": "resource_utilization",
            "generated_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            "department": department or "all",
            "summary": {
                "total_team_members": len(users),
                "total_capacity_percent": total_capacity,
                "total_utilized_percent": total_used,
                "utilization_rate": round((total_used / total_capacity * 100) if total_capacity > 0 else 0, 1),
                "underutilized_count": len(underutilized),
                "overutilized_count": len(overutilized),
            },
            "details": users,
            "underutilized": underutilized,
            "overutilized": overutilized,
        }

    def performance_metrics_report(self, user_id: str = None, days: int = 30) -> dict:
        cutoff = (datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=days)).isoformat()

        if user_id:
            tasks_completed = self.db.execute(
                """SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE assigned_to = ? AND status = 'done'
                   AND completed_at >= ?""",
                (user_id, cutoff),
            )
            tasks_total = self.db.execute(
                "SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM tasks WHERE assigned_to = ?", (user_id,)
            )
            activities = self.db.execute(
                "SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM activity_log WHERE user_id = ? AND created_at >= ?",
                (user_id, cutoff),
            )
        else:
            tasks_completed = self.db.execute(
                "SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE status = 'done' AND completed_at >= ?",
                (cutoff,),
            )
            tasks_total = self.db.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM tasks")
            activities = self.db.execute(
                "SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM activity_log WHERE created_at >= ?",
                (cutoff,),
            )

        on_time = sum(1 for t in tasks_completed if not t["due_date"] or t["completed_at"] <= t["due_date"])
        total_completed = len(tasks_completed)
        avg_hours = (
            sum(t["actual_hours"] or 0 for t in tasks_completed) / total_completed
            if total_completed > 0 else 0
        )

        return {
            "report_type": "performance_metrics",
            "generated_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            "period_days": days,
            "user_id": user_id or "all",
            "metrics": {
                "tasks_completed": total_completed,
                "total_tasks": tasks_total[0]["count"] if tasks_total else 0,
                "on_time_delivery_rate": round((on_time / total_completed * 100) if total_completed > 0 else 0, 1),
                "avg_hours_per_task": round(avg_hours, 1),
                "activity_count": activities[0]["count"] if activities else 0,
            },
        }

    def department_summary_report(self) -> dict:
        departments = self.db.execute(
            "SELECT DISTINCT department FROM users WHERE department IS NOT NULL AND department != ''"
        )
        summaries = []
        for dept in departments:
            dept_name = dept["department"]
            members = self.db.execute(
                "SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM users WHERE department = ? AND is_active = 1",
                (dept_name,),
            )
            projects = self.db.execute(
                "SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM projects WHERE department = ?", (dept_name,)
            )
            active_tasks = self.db.execute(
                """SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM tasks t
                   JOIN users u ON t.assigned_to = u.id
                   WHERE u.department = ? AND t.status != 'done'""",
                (dept_name,),
            )
            summaries.append({
                "department": dept_name,
                "member_count": members[0]["count"],
                "project_count": projects[0]["count"],
                "active_tasks": active_tasks[0]["count"],
            })

        return {
            "report_type": "department_summary",
            "generated_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            "departments": summaries,
        }

class APIResponse:
    """Standard API response wrapper."""

    @staticmethod
    def success(data: Any = None, message: str = "Success", status: int = 200) -> dict:
        return {"status": status, "message": message, "data": data, "success": True}

    @staticmethod
    def error(message: str = "Error", status: int = 400, details: Any = None) -> dict:
        return {"status": status, "message": message, "data": details, "success": False}


class TeamSyncProAPI:
    """RESTful API router handling all endpoint dispatch and request processing."""

    def __init__(self, app: "TeamSyncPro"):
        self.app = app
        self._routes: dict[str, dict] = {}
        self._register_routes()

    def _register_routes(self):
        self._routes = {
            "POST /auth/register": self._register,
            "POST /auth/login": self._login,
            "POST /auth/logout": self._logout,
            "POST /projects": self._create_project,
            "GET /projects": self._list_projects,
            "GET /projects/{id}": self._get_project,
            "PUT /projects/{id}": self._update_project,
            "DELETE /projects/{id}": self._delete_project,
            "GET /projects/{id}/summary": self._get_project_summary,
            "POST /tasks": self._create_task,
            "GET /tasks/{id}": self._get_task,
            "PUT /tasks/{id}": self._update_task,
            "DELETE /tasks/{id}": self._delete_task,
            "GET /projects/{id}/tasks": self._get_project_tasks,
            "GET /users/{id}/tasks": self._get_user_tasks,
            "POST /resources/allocate": self._allocate_resource,
            "DELETE /resources/{id}": self._deallocate_resource,
            "GET /users/{id}/availability": self._get_availability,
            "GET /resources/workload": self._get_workload,
            "POST /messages": self._send_message,
            "GET /messages": self._get_messages,
            "GET /notifications": self._get_notifications,
            "POST /integrations": self._add_integration,
            "GET /integrations": self._get_integrations,
            "GET /reports/project/{id}": self._project_report,
            "GET /reports/resources": self._resource_report,
            "GET /reports/performance": self._performance_report,
            "GET /reports/departments": self._department_report,
        }

    def handle_request(self, method: str, path: str, body: dict = None,
                       headers: dict = None) -> dict:
        body = body or {}
        headers = headers or {}
        route_key = f"{method.upper()} {path}"

        handler = self._routes.get(route_key)
        if not handler:
            for pattern, h in self._routes.items():
                if self._match_route(pattern, route_key):
                    handler = h
                    break

        if not handler:
            return APIResponse.error("Endpoint not found", 404)

        try:
            return handler(body=body, headers=headers, path=path)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"API error on {route_key}: {e}")
            return APIResponse.error(str(e), 500)

    def _match_route(self, pattern: str, actual: str) -> bool:
        p_parts = pattern.split("/")
        a_parts = actual.split("/")
        if len(p_parts) != len(a_parts):
            return False
        for p, a in zip(p_parts, a_parts):
            if p.startswith("{") and p.endswith("}"):
                continue
            if p != a:
                return False
        return True

    def _extract_path_param(self, path: str, position: int = -1) -> str:
        parts = path.strip("/").split("/")
        return parts[position] if abs(position) <= len(parts) else ""

    def _auth_check(self, headers: dict, resource: str, action: str) -> Optional[dict]:
        token = headers.get("Authorization", "").replace("Bearer ", "")
        if not token:
            return APIResponse.error("Authentication required", 401)
        if not self.app.auth.check_permission(token, resource, action):
            return APIResponse.error("Insufficient permissions", 403)
        return None

    def _get_user_id(self, headers: dict) -> str:
        token = headers.get("Authorization", "").replace("Bearer ", "")
        session = self.app.auth.get_session(token)
        return session["user_id"] if session else ""

    # Auth endpoints
    def _register(self, body: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:
        result = self.app.auth.register_user(
            username=body.get("username", ""),
            email=body.get("email", ""),
            password=body.get("password", ""),
            full_name=body.get("full_name", ""),
            role=body.get("role", "member"),
            department=body.get("department", ""),
        )
        if result["success"]:
            return APIResponse.success(result, "User registered", 201)
        return APIResponse.error(result["error"])

    def _login(self, body: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:
        result = self.app.auth.login(body.get("username", ""), body.get("password", ""))
        if result["success"]:
            return APIResponse.success(result, "Login successful")
        return APIResponse.error(result["error"], 401)

    def _logout(self, headers: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:
        token = headers.get("Authorization", "").replace("Bearer ", "")
        self.app.auth.logout(token)
        return APIResponse.success(message="Logged out")

    # Project endpoints
    def _create_project(self, body: dict, headers: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "projects", "create")
        if err:
            return err
        result = self.app.projects.create_project(
            name=body.get("name", ""),
            owner_id=self._get_user_id(headers),
            description=body.get("description", ""),
            priority=body.get("priority", "medium"),
            department=body.get("department", ""),
            start_date=body.get("start_date", ""),
            end_date=body.get("end_date", ""),
            budget=body.get("budget", 0.0),
        )
        return APIResponse.success(result, "Project created", 201)

    def _list_projects(self, body: dict, headers: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "projects", "read")
        if err:
            return err
        projects = self.app.projects.list_projects(
            department=body.get("department"),
            status=body.get("status"),
        )
        return APIResponse.success(projects)

    def _get_project(self, headers: dict, path: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "projects", "read")
        if err:
            return err
        project_id = self._extract_path_param(path, 1)
        project = self.app.projects.get_project(project_id)
        if project:
            return APIResponse.success(project)
        return APIResponse.error("Project not found", 404)

    def _update_project(self, body: dict, headers: dict, path: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "projects", "update")
        if err:
            return err
        project_id = self._extract_path_param(path, 1)
        result = self.app.projects.update_project(project_id, **body)
        return APIResponse.success(result)

    def _delete_project(self, headers: dict, path: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "projects", "delete")
        if err:
            return err
        project_id = self._extract_path_param(path, 1)
        result = self.app.projects.delete_project(project_id)
        return APIResponse.success(result)

    def _get_project_summary(self, headers: dict, path: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "projects", "read")
        if err:
            return err
        parts = path.strip("/").split("/")
        project_id = parts[1] if len(parts) > 1 else ""
        summary = self.app.projects.get_project_summary(project_id)
        return APIResponse.success(summary)

    # Task endpoints
    def _create_task(self, body: dict, headers: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "tasks", "create")
        if err:
            return err
        result = self.app.tasks.create_task(
            project_id=body.get("project_id", ""),
            title=body.get("title", ""),
            created_by=self._get_user_id(headers),
            description=body.get("description", ""),
            priority=body.get("priority", "medium"),
            assigned_to=body.get("assigned_to", ""),
            estimated_hours=body.get("estimated_hours", 0.0),
            due_date=body.get("due_date", ""),
        )
        return APIResponse.success(result, "Task created", 201)

    def _get_task(self, headers: dict, path: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "tasks", "read")
        if err:
            return err
        task_id = self._extract_path_param(path, 1)
        task = self.app.tasks.get_task(task_id)
        if task:
            return APIResponse.success(task)
        return APIResponse.error("Task not found", 404)

    def _update_task(self, body: dict, headers: dict, path: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "tasks", "update")
        if err:
            return err
        task_id = self._extract_path_param(path, 1)
        result = self.app.tasks.update_task(task_id, self._get_user_id(headers), **body)
        return APIResponse.success(result)

    def _delete_task(self, headers: dict, path: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "tasks", "delete")
        if err:
            return err
        task_id = self._extract_path_param(path, 1)
        result = self.app.tasks.delete_task(task_id, self._get_user_id(headers))
        return APIResponse.success(result)

    def _get_project_tasks(self, body: dict, headers: dict, path: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "tasks", "read")
        if err:
            return err
        parts = path.strip("/").split("/")
        project_id = parts[1] if len(parts) > 2 else ""
        tasks = self.app.tasks.get_tasks_by_project(project_id, body.get("status"))
        return APIResponse.success(tasks)

    def _get_user_tasks(self, body: dict, headers: dict, path: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "tasks", "read")
        if err:
            return err
        parts = path.strip("/").split("/")
        user_id = parts[1] if len(parts) > 2 else ""
        tasks = self.app.tasks.get_tasks_by_user(user_id, body.get("status"))
        return APIResponse.success(tasks)

    # Resource endpoints
    def _allocate_resource(self, body: dict, headers: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "projects", "update")
        if err:
            return err
        result = self.app.resources.allocate_resource(
            user_id=body.get("user_id", ""),
            project_id=body.get("project_id", ""),
            allocation_percent=body.get("allocation_percent", 100.0),
            role_in_project=body.get("role_in_project", ""),
            start_date=body.get("start_date", ""),
            end_date=body.get("end_date", ""),
        )
        return APIResponse.success(result)

    def _deallocate_resource(self, headers: dict, path: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "projects", "update")
        if err:
            return err
        resource_id = self._extract_path_param(path, 1)
        result = self.app.resources.deallocate_resource(resource_id)
        return APIResponse.success(result)

    def _get_availability(self, headers: dict, path: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "users", "read")
        if err:
            return err
        parts = path.strip("/").split("/")
        user_id = parts[1] if len(parts) > 2 else ""
        availability = self.app.resources.get_availability(user_id)
        return APIResponse.success(availability)

    def _get_workload(self, headers: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "reports", "read")
        if err:
            return err
        workload = self.app.resources.get_workload_report()
        return APIResponse.success(workload)

    # Communication endpoints
    def _send_message(self, body: dict, headers: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "messages", "create")
        if err:
            return err
        result = self.app.communication.send_message(
            sender_id=self._get_user_id(headers),
            content=body.get("content", ""),
            project_id=body.get("project_id", ""),
            recipient_id=body.get("recipient_id", ""),
            channel=body.get("channel", "general"),
        )
        return APIResponse.success(result, "Message sent", 201)

    def _get_messages(self, body: dict, headers: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "messages", "read")
        if err:
            return err
        messages = self.app.communication.get_messages(
            project_id=body.get("project_id", ""),
            channel=body.get("channel", "general"),
            limit=body.get("limit", 50),
        )
        return APIResponse.success(messages)

    def _get_notifications(self, headers: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:
        user_id = self._get_user_id(headers)
        notifications = self.app.communication.get_notifications(user_id, unread_only=True)
        return APIResponse.success(notifications)

    # Integration endpoints
    def _add_integration(self, body: dict, headers: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "integrations", "create")
        if err:
            return err
        result = self.app.integrations.add_integration(
            user_id=self._get_user_id(headers),
            service_type=body.get("service_type", ""),
            config=body.get("config", {}),
        )
        return APIResponse.success(result, "Integration added", 201)

    def _get_integrations(self, headers: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "integrations", "read")
        if err:
            return err
        integrations = self.app.integrations.get_integrations(self._get_user_id(headers))
        return APIResponse.success(integrations)

    # Report endpoints
    def _project_report(self, headers: dict, path: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "reports", "read")
        if err:
            return err
        parts = path.strip("/").split("/")
        project_id = parts[2] if len(parts) > 2 else ""
        report = self.app.reporting.project_progress_report(project_id)
        return APIResponse.success(report)

    def _resource_report(self, body: dict, headers: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:

        report = self.app.reporting.performance_metrics_report(

        )
        return APIResponse.success(report)

    def _department_report(self, headers: dict, **kwargs) -> dict:
        err = self._auth_check(headers, "reports", "read")
        if err:
            return err
        report = self.app.reporting.department_summary_report()
        return APIResponse.success(report)

class FrontendRenderer:
    """Generates user-facing interface views with role-based content filtering."""

    def __init__(self, app: "TeamSyncPro"):
        self.app = app

    def render_dashboard(self, user_id: str, role: str) -> dict:
        user_tasks = self.app.tasks.get_tasks_by_user(user_id)
        active_tasks = [t for t in user_tasks if t["status"] != "done"]
        unread = self.app.communication.get_unread_count(user_id)
        availability = self.app.resources.get_availability(user_id)

        dashboard = {
            "view": "dashboard",
            "user_id": user_id,
            "role": role,
            "widgets": {
                "my_tasks": {
                    "total": len(user_tasks),
                    "active": len(active_tasks),
                    "items": active_tasks[:5],
                },
                "unread_messages": unread,
                "availability": availability,
            },
        }

        if role in (UserRole.ADMIN.value, UserRole.MANAGER.value):
            dashboard["widgets"]["team_workload"] = self.app.resources.get_workload_report()
            projects = self.app.projects.list_projects(status="active")
            dashboard["widgets"]["active_projects"] = {
                "count": len(projects),
                "items": projects[:5],
            }

        return dashboard

    def render_project_board(self, project_id: str) -> dict:
        tasks = self.app.tasks.get_tasks_by_project(project_id)
        columns = {
            "todo": [],
            "in_progress": [],
            "in_review": [],
            "done": [],
            "blocked": [],
        }
        for task in tasks:
            status = task["status"]
            if status in columns:
                columns[status].append(task)

        project = self.app.projects.get_project(project_id)
        team = self.app.resources.get_project_team(project_id)

        return {
            "view": "project_board",
            "project": project,
            "columns": columns,
            "team": team,
        }

    def render_task_detail(self, task_id: str) -> dict:
        task = self.app.tasks.get_task(task_id)
        if not task:
            return {"error": "Task not found"}
        dependencies = self.app.tasks.get_dependencies(task_id)
        return {"view": "task_detail", "task": task, "dependencies": dependencies}

    def render_reports_page(self, role: str) -> dict:
        available_reports = ["project_progress", "performance_metrics"]
        if role in (UserRole.ADMIN.value, UserRole.MANAGER.value):
            available_reports.extend(["resource_utilization", "department_summary"])
        return {"view": "reports", "available_reports": available_reports}

class TeamSyncPro:
    """Main application orchestrator integrating all services."""

    def __init__(self, db_path: str = ":memory:"):
        self.db = Database(db_path)
        self.auth = AuthService(self.db)
        self.tasks = TaskManager(self.db)
        self.projects = ProjectManager(self.db)
        self.resources = ResourceManager(self.db)
        self.communication = CommunicationService(self.db)
        self.integrations = IntegrationService(self.db)
        self.reporting = ReportingService(self.db)
        self.api = TeamSyncProAPI(self)
        self.frontend = FrontendRenderer(self)
        logger.info("TeamSyncPro initialized successfully")

    def request(self, method: str, path: str, body: dict = None,
                headers: dict = None) -> dict:
        return self.api.handle_request(method, path, body, headers)
